# PayoPoint Slide Catalog

Read this file when choosing slide types for the deck plan.

## Core Narrative Slides

- `title-slide`: opening slide with title, subtitle, presenter, and date
- `agenda-slide`: agenda or session map for longer meetings
- `content-slide`: general-purpose slide for concise bullets or short sections
- `section-divider-slide`: visual break between major parts of the story
- `closing-slide`: Q&A or thank-you ending
- `next-steps-slide`: actions, owners, dates, and follow-ups

## Decision And Comparison Slides

- `problem-slide`: define the pain points, risks, or current-state issues
- `table-slide`: compare options, criteria, or feature sets
- `pros-cons-slide`: frame tradeoffs in a simpler two-sided format
- `milestone-slide`: highlight a win, launch, or completed phase

## Data And Evidence Slides

- `stats-slide`: a small number of headline metrics
- `graph-slide`: trend or distribution chart with supporting takeaways
- `quote-slide`: testimonial, endorsement, or leadership statement
- `resources-slide`: links, QR codes, references, or follow-up materials

## Process And Technical Slides

- `process-slide`: ordered steps, lifecycle, or workflow
- `sequence-process-slide`: left-to-right workflow with labeled panels and centered chevron separators
- `architecture-slide`: system diagram or platform view with callouts
- `team-slide`: team ownership, stakeholders, or org view
- `icon-grid-slide`: capabilities, components, or feature set summary

## Visual Impact Slides

- `image-slide`: visual hero slide with minimal supporting copy
- `video-slide`: embedded or linked demo framing slide

## Selection Rules

- Prefer `content-slide` when the content is mostly explanatory text.
- Prefer `stats-slide` or `graph-slide` when the slide needs proof, not more bullets.
- Prefer `problem-slide`, `table-slide`, or `pros-cons-slide` for decision decks.
- Prefer `architecture-slide`, `process-slide`, and `icon-grid-slide` for technical audiences.
- Prefer `sequence-process-slide` when the story needs 4-6 explicit stages with short panel copy and visible left-to-right progression.
- If the requested slide idea does not exactly match a template, choose the closest existing type and call out the approximation in the final summary.
